channel.title: AISight — AI Capital Expenditure Tracker
channel.description: Latest news and data on AI infrastructure spending across 38 companies. Updated 3x daily from SEC filings and earnings calls.

item.title: Tencent’s First-Quarter Profit Jumps 21% as Capex Tops USD4.4 Billion on AI Spending - Yicai Global

item.title: Microsoft’s Massive Kenya AI Data Center Blocked By ‘Half the Country’ Power Demand
item.description: A planned $1 billion artificial intelligence data center in Kenya, spearheaded by Microsoft and Abu Dhabi-based AI firm G42, has stalled due to disagreements over energy guarantees. The project has encountered delays after the Kenyan government declined to me… [Microsoft, Intel]

item.title: The most-cited computer scientist alive says AI could make humanity extinct within a decade
item.description: Yoshua Bengio, the Turing Award-winning computer scientist widely regarded as one of the godfathers of artificial intelligence, has renewed his warning that hyperintelligent machines could pose an existential threat to humanity within the next decade. In an i… [Intel]

item.title: Institutional investors flocked to establish new stakes in semiconductor firms in first quarter
item.description: Institutional investors actively bought shares in semiconductor companies such as Micron and Intel during the first quarter. This move positioned them to benefit from a significant market rally. Demand for memory chips, fueled by AI development, drove substan… [Intel]

item.title: Amazon vs Walmart: Great American race begins for $1 trillion annual sales. How will this benefit customers in rural U.S?
item.description: Amazon last year invested $4 billion to bring same-day or next-day deliveries to 4,000 smaller cities, towns and rural communities. [Amazon]

item.title: Cerebras' blockbuster IPO boosts hype for SpaceX and OpenAI, but crowds out smaller players
item.description: After Cerebras popped in its market debut, all attention turns to SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic, which are already among the most valuable U.S. tech companies. [OpenAI, Anthropic]

item.title: Walmart’s upper hand over Amazon in the $1 trillion e-commerce race: 90% of Americans live within 10 miles of a superstore
item.description: Last month, Andy Jassy said the average monthly number of Amazon customers receiving same-day deliveries doubled in 2025 compared to the year before. [Amazon]

item.title: SpaceX, OpenAI, and Anthropic: Here are the most anticipated IPOs in 2026
item.description: Chipmaker Cerberus just debuted as 2026's biggest IPO, but it may soon be dwarfed by other tech giants. [OpenAI, Anthropic]

item.title: Why Amazon Put Down Rufus, Its Loyal AI Assistant
item.description: Here lies Amazon’s Rufus, a loyal if annoying AI assistant. Born in the LLM boom of 2024. Died unloved and underused in 2026. We hardly knew or wanted thee. John Herrman explains why. [Amazon]

item.title: Meta Stock 2026: Buy Recommendation as AI and Advertising Drive Momentum
item.description: Meta Platforms is poised for growth in 2026, driven by AI advancements, robust advertising revenue, and expanding user engagement, with analysts maintaining strong buy ratings despite valuation concerns. [Meta]

item.title: Tata Electronics, ASML join hands for semiconductor manufacturing in India
item.description: Tata Electronics and ASML are joining forces to establish chip manufacturing in India. This partnership will support Tata Electronics' new semiconductor Fab in Dholera, Gujarat. ASML's advanced lithography tools will be deployed. The collaboration aims to acc…

item.title: This Company Was an American Success Story. Until MAHA Influencers Sank It.
item.description: James Rogers, the former CEO of Apeel Sciences, is seen at its headquarters in Santa Barbara, California. (Yuri Hasegawa/Redux) All it took was two Facebook posts to turn an online mob against Apeel Sciences and its booming business of keeping food fresh long… [Meta]

item.title: LA’s $30 minimum wage pushes hotel owners to breaking point
item.description: Hotel owners in Los Angeles told The Post the city’s latest decision to bump the minimum wage to $30-an-hour for hotel workers is the “final nail in the coffin” for the industry. …

item.title: We watched social media concentrate. The same thing is happening in AI, only at a deeper layer
item.description: We watched Snap lose to Meta's platform dominance and see the same pattern repeating in AI—only this time, the stakes aren't market share. [Meta]

item.title: I’ve been studying Big Tech for a long time. What just happened with Anthropic and the Pentagon terrifies me
item.description: For years, I watched regulators fail to keep pace with Silicon Valley. The Anthropic-Pentagon standoff is proof we've run out of time. [Anthropic]

item.title: Snap, YouTube, and TikTok settle school addiction lawsuit, leaving Meta to face trial alone
item.description: Snap, Google’s YouTube, and ByteDance’s TikTok have reached settlements in the first lawsuit brought by a public school district over claims that social media addiction has disrupted learning and forced schools to spend heavily on combating a youth mental hea… [Meta, ByteDance, Amazon, Alphabet]
